validation_outcome: Repo-root `make` builds clean (exit 0). Test build (`cd tests && make`) links clean (exit 0). Top-level suite `./SSWTests` (cwd = tests/) reports OK (217 tests), all passing, confirmed green across 4 consecutive runs with the fix in place. No production-code test seam was required: checkForVictory()'s private case-4/case-5 branches were driven end-to-end through the public FGame::endUPFTurn() flow with the retreat condition selected via a mock IStrategicUI, station/ship destruction staged through the real cleanUpShips() combat path, UPF-win outcomes observed via the production notifyVictory(1) callback, and counter resets read back from the real FGame::save() byte stream. Regression proof: temporarily re-swapping the case bodies in src/strategic/FGame.cpp (reproducing the pre-fix defect) caused all 6 counter-driven tests to FAIL (case-4 expected-1/actual-0, case-4 no-win expected-0/actual-2, case-4 regression expected-0/actual-1, case-5 expected-0/actual-3, case-5 no-win expected-0/actual-1, case-5 regression expected-1/actual-0); restoring the fix returns them all to green. FGame.cpp is confirmed back to the committed fix and only the two test files are modified.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.evaluated_condition_matches_displayed_te: COVERED (testShowRetreatConditionsTextMatchesEvaluatedCaseNumber, plus the counter-driven tests tie each case number's evaluation to the correct counters)
acceptance_criteria_coverage.planner_mandated_swapped_code_regression: COVERED (testCheckForVictoryCondition4RegressionDoesNotAwardVictoryWhenStationsAtFiveDespiteShipLossImbalance and testCheckForVictoryCondition5RegressionAwardsVictoryWhenStationsAtFiveDespiteBeingAboveThreshold; both proven to fail against pre-fix code)

notes: Pre-existing flaky test flagged for coordinator awareness (out of CRIT-2 scope, no action taken): FFleetTest::testDecTransitTime failed once during the swapped-code regression run. Root cause is a time-seeded RNG risk-jump: FFleet::decTransitTime() rolls irand(100) > getRJChance(); the test fleet (speed 2, no battleship) has getRJChance() == 90, so the jump fails ~9% of the time. irand uses the global RNG seeded by srand(time(NULL)) in the FGame constructor, so the pass/fail depends on the wall-clock second. It is unrelated to the CRIT-2 fix and to the added tests; the full suite passed cleanly on 4 consecutive runs with the fix.
